title: Multilingual patient-facing cards
context: Backlog/future: multilingual renditions of the patient-facing plain-language cards. HIGH risk-tier and education-only (not medical advice); coordinate with / defer to the ewing-info-translations project, with per-language oncologist + patient-advocate review. Languages are NOT yet enumerated and depend on a secured partner/community, so this is one representative task that expands per-language on scope confirmation - no languages are fabricated. Binding cancer guardrails apply: only open-access / aggregate / de-identified sources (controlled-access - dbGaP, EGA, individual-level biobanks - and any identifiable patient data are out of scope); per-source license verified before reuse (COSMIC/OncoKB cite-only, CIViC CC0, DepMap CC-BY, ClinVar public domain, PMC-OA per-article); no-source-no-claim provenance on every assertion. No partner/reviewer/steward is yet secured, so this task carries requestor 'TO BE SECURED' and verifiedNeed false.
objective: Produce multilingual patient-facing card renditions (representative task; languages expand on partner/scope confirmation), each translated faithfully from its published parent plain-language card, carrying the not-medical-advice frame, with per-language oncologist + patient-advocate sign-off, coordinated with ewing-info-translations to avoid duplication.
acceptanceCriteria: Each translated card is faithful to its published parent plain-language card and carries the persistent 'research information, not medical advice' frame in the target language
Per-language oncologist + patient-advocate sign-off recorded; cards withheld if either sign-off is missing or stale (reviewers TO BE SECURED)
Coordinated with / deferred to ewing-info-translations to avoid duplication; target languages enumerated only once a partner/community confirms scope (no languages fabricated)
Translation output license is source-compatible with the parent CC-BY-4.0 cards (no relicensing of copyrighted source content)
